confidence_meta.reason: Active Iran-Israel war escalation / Strait of Hormuz strikes within 48h

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Geopolitical Oil Shock + AI-Chip Correction (Liquidity Shock / Inflation Reaccel risk)
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Energy, value, defensives over semis/Korea beta; short-duration favored

bias: Structural plumbing continues to ease (TGA draining, reserves rising, net liquidity +$148B) even as today's price action deteriorates sharply on an oil shock and a Korea/AI-chip unwind — a liquidity tailwind colliding with a geopolitical and earnings-driven risk-off shock.

counterargs.thesis: Liquidity easing supports risk assets despite today's shock
supporting: Reserves rose to $3.099T, RRP near zero — ample reserve supply
SOFR down 7bps to 3.55%, no funding stress
HY OAS flat at 2.69% — credit spreads unstressed
USDKRW still below 1,530 BOK watch line
against: Oil +7.34% single-day on US-Iran Strait of Hormuz strikes
Entire UST curve up 0.9-1.5% intraday — bear-steepening
Korea gate closed 1/3 — EWY and KOSPI both fail technical levels
counterargs.read: The plumbing backdrop (TGA drainage, rising reserves, falling SOFR) is genuinely liquidity-friendly and would normally argue for risk-on. But today's tape is being driven by two exogenous shocks — a Strait of Hormuz oil-supply scare and an SK Hynix/Samsung earnings-and-valuation unwind in the AI-chip trade — that override the plumbing signal in the near term. Until oil stabilizes and the Korea/semis complex finds a floor, the liquidity tailwind is necessary but not sufficient for risk assets to turn.

trade_table.rationale: Korea gate closed 1/3; SK Hynix -15.4%, Samsung -10.7%, SOXX -5.0% on HBM pricing/earnings fears — trend and momentum both broken.
Iran-US Strait of Hormuz escalation; +7.34% today, headline risk skewed higher near-term.
Entire curve backed up today (US10Y +1.16%) despite risk-off — oil-driven inflation fear offsetting flight-to-quality bid.
HY OAS flat, financials firm — no credit contagion yet from the equity shock.
-3.06% despite geopolitical risk-off — likely dollar strength (DXY +0.28%) and profit-taking after recent highs.

catalysts.event: Iran-Israel war escalation / Strait of Hormuz strikes
catalysts.note: US reimposing blockade on Iranian vessels; Kuwait offshore platform hit — active supply-shock risk.
KIS research flags operating profit may miss consensus by ~8% on HBM pricing.
No SEP this meeting; oil shock complicates the inflation read into this decision.

duration_score.reason: Nominal yields rose across the curve alongside a sharp equity/vol shock — an unusual combination where an oil-supply scare is pushing yields up even as risk assets sell off, rather than the classic flight-to-quality bid. Real 5Y (DFII5 1.99%) has not repriced yet, so this looks more like a headline-driven yield backup than a durable regime shift.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Oil-driven inflation risk is outweighing the flight-to-quality bid despite a sharp equity selloff
james_framework.pain_trade: Rising nominal yields into a risk-off equity shock — duration offers no hedge today
james_framework.watch_signal: If Strait of Hormuz risk de-escalates and yields fall back while equities stabilize, treat today's yield backup as transient; if yields keep rising into further equity weakness, that's a genuine stagflationary shock

dfii5.label: 5Y TIPS real yield (primary)
dfii5.note: -0.01%, the primary market-based real-rate signal — not yet reacting to the oil shock.

real_yield_2y_proxy.note: Modeled proxy only, not central to the read.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: Breakevens (T5YIE/T10YIE) have not moved despite a >7% oil spike — markets are treating the Strait of Hormuz shock as a supply-side headline risk rather than a durable inflation regime shift, for now.

spread_2s10s.note: 0.343% vs 0.359% prior — mild flattening as front end leads.

chain.note: Memory ETF hit hard on SK Hynix/Samsung HBM pricing fears.
SK Hynix — record one-day drop; KIS flags op. profit may miss consensus ~8%.
Samsung Electronics — falling alongside SK Hynix on AI-chip rotation/earnings fears.

sector_rotation.current_regime: Geopolitical Oil Shock + AI-Chip Unwind (mixed Liquidity Shock / Inflation Reaccel)

actionable_output.note: Signals are genuinely mixed today: easing Fed plumbing argues for liquidity expansion, but the oil shock and Korea/AI-chip unwind are pricing more like a liquidity-shock/inflation-reaccel blend. Wait for oil and KOSPI to stabilize before leaning into the plumbing-driven bull case.
